Acceptance complete for HXC-SCAF-002, HXC-SCAF-006, and HXC-SCAF-007. Haxe 4.3.7 at e0b355c6be312c1b17382603f018cf52522ec651, Reflaxe 4.0.0-beta-compatible commit 73a983112e039daad46b37912ab238df6bf0cf53/tree 05ab4abc670b6e3ed5424e78c622b92979b5affb, its 62-file SHA-256 bundle, and Lix 17.0.2 npm integrity are locked by .haxerc, package-lock.json, docs/specs/toolchain-lock.json, and provenance policy. Compiler-only Reflaxe is vendored with its MIT license and is not hxrt. npm test passed exact-tool verification, cold source probes, reversed-order negative diagnostics, duplicate idempotence, back-to-back C/non-C/C compiler-server isolation, an unrelated-CWD staged-package mount, package notices, license policy, and 8 governance tests. jq empty docs/specs/*.json .haxerc haxelib.json package.json package-lock.json passed; bd dep cycles --json returned []; bd lint --json returned zero issues; git diff --check passed; diff -qr/q proved vendor/reflaxe/{src,LICENSE,haxelib.json} matches the selected upstream checkout. docs/toolchain.md, README, PRD, research notes, AGENTS, CI, and pre-commit policy document and enforce the pin/update and bootstrap-before-init contracts. Full adapter typing/HXC1000 remains E0.T04; target.name/stdlib conflict coverage remains haxe_c-od2.4.

Acceptance complete for the M0 contract slice of HXC-MAC-001, HXC-MAC-002, HXC-MAC-003, HXC-MAC-007, HXC-MAC-010, HXC-MAC-011, and HXC-MAC-012. Added 42 method-free std/c compiler contracts; canonical namespaced @:c.* metadata; per-compilation TypedCContractSnapshot/TypedCContractMacro with structural type refs, forward/complete declaration edges, normalized/deduplicated build facts, explicit effects, and HXC5002 validation; admitted compile-time-only c.StaticAssert.require while c.Syntax/c.Unsafe remain non-operational. docs/typed-c-authoring.md, ADR 0002, PRD §17/§32, architecture, configuration, research, README, AGENTS, durable Beads vocabulary, and downstream live issue notes now agree. Positive snapshot plus 11 negative fixtures prove typed enum/literal admission, paths/build names, reserved/duplicate symbols, pack shape, opaque by-value rejection, cycle rejection, missing args, static assertions, root-order determinism, valid/invalid/valid compiler-server isolation, and empty allocation/unsafe/runtime effects. Evidence: npm test; jq empty docs/specs/*.json .haxerc haxelib.json package.json package-lock.json; python3 -m py_compile test/typed_c/run.py test/bootstrap/run.py; bd dep cycles --json => []; bd lint --json => zero; bash scripts/lint/whitespace_guard.sh and --staged; git diff --check; targeted trailing-whitespace scan. Actual c.* lowering, generated headers/native assertions, Clang ABI authority, public ABI stability, hxc inspection, and whole-program runtime plans remain with the documented E0/E1/E3/E4/E6/E7/E8/E9/E10 owners.

Acceptance complete for HXC-SCAF-003, HXC-SCAF-007, and HXC-SEM-006 target-identity validation. CompilerBootstrap/CompilerInit now expose deterministic count=1 markers, validate the raw Haxe carrier before normalizing target.name=c, preserve c_output as transport and c as the public conditional, retain reflaxe_c as implementation-only, add target.unicode without adding capability flags, and issue HXC0003 at the compilation root for conflicting identity, UTF-16, unproven atomics, or freestanding carrier facts. test/bootstrap/TargetContractProbe.hx plus expected/target-contract.json structurally prove the pinned upstream String.hx, StringTools.hx, and UnicodeString.hx scalar branches, target.utf16=false/utf16=false, target.name=c, public/internal visibility, c_output and compiler-owned target.name activation, caller -D c non-activation, non-C isolation, duplicate idempotence, package/CWD behavior, and valid/invalid/valid compiler-server isolation. The experiment also proved a material pin constraint: real Haxe 4.3.7 Cross predefines target.name=cross and unremovable target.utf16/utf16 before macros. Production Cross now fails closed instead of typing the wrong program; new P0 decision haxe_c-od2.6 evaluates a pinned Haxe 4 patch, a supported custom-target Haxe version, or a superseding compatibility choice and blocks E0.T04/E4.T03. ADR 0004/0007, PRD, architecture, configuration, toolchain, research, README, AGENTS, diagnostic catalog, CI, pre-commit, and live downstream notes agree that Eval is a test carrier only and its sys/threaded facts are not C capability claims. Evidence: npm test; npm run test:bootstrap after final formatting; jq empty docs/specs/*.json test/bootstrap/expected/target-contract.json .haxerc haxelib.json package.json package-lock.json; python3 -m py_compile test/bootstrap/run.py test/typed_c/run.py; bd dep cycles --json => []; bd lint --json => zero; bash scripts/lint/whitespace_guard.sh; git diff --check.

Accepted exact official Haxe 5.0.0-preview.1 at revision 2c1e544e0a2c7524ef4c8e103f1b0580362ea538 as the production typing carrier. Direct builds use --custom-target c=<output>; automatic c.Init.init()/TargetPlatform configure a static, block-scoped, scalar-Unicode PlatformConfig before typing, derive sys only for hosted, and advertise no threads/atomics without adapter evidence. CompilerInit validates that snapshot and derives Reflaxe c_output from Compiler.getOutput(), rejecting missing or mismatched output. Actual non-probe registration reaches source-root HXC1000 and emits no artifact in cold and compiler-server builds; lifecycle, staged-package, non-C isolation, recovery, hosted/freestanding, and upstream String/StringTools/UnicodeString snapshots pass. ADR 0007, ADR 0004, PRD, README, AGENTS, architecture, configuration, toolchain, provenance, research, and checked plan now agree. Rejected Haxe 4 Cross because unremovable UTF-16 facts leak to application conditionals; rejected a Haxe 4 fork because of cross-host distribution/maintenance burden; retained Eval only for future hxc bootstrap, differential oracle, and isolation. Exact official platform artifact hashes are lock-checked. Evidence: npm test; npm run deps:verify; jq empty docs/specs/*.json; python3 -m py_compile scripts/ci/check_toolchain.py test/bootstrap/run.py test/typed_c/run.py; bash scripts/lint/whitespace_guard.sh; bd lint --json (0); bd dep cycles --json ([]); git diff --check.
